name: Remiel
description: Remiel, whose name translates to "Thunder of God" or "Mercy of God," is an archangel primarily found within apocryphal and pseudepigraphal Jewish and Christian texts. He is not typically recognized within mainstream canonical scriptures. Remiel's role is often associated with overseeing the resurrection of the dead and guiding souls to judgment. He is also connected with divine visions and is sometimes identified as the angel who presides over those who rise. His character is complex, displaying both aspects of divine judgment and mercy. Remiel embodies the duality inherent in the divine, reflecting both the awesome power and the compassionate nature of God. While not as prominent as other archangels like Michael or Gabriel, Remiel holds a significant place in certain esoteric traditions, representing hope and the promise of renewal beyond earthly existence. He is a figure of contemplation, inviting reflection on mortality, judgment, and the potential for redemption.
subtitle: The Angel of Resurrection

keyMyths.title: Remiel and the Resurrection
keyMyths.description: Remiel's primary role is to oversee the resurrection of the dead. He is believed to sound the trumpet that signals the beginning of the end times, calling forth the souls of the deceased to face judgment. This role positions him as a pivotal figure in eschatological narratives, embodying the promise of eternal life and the consequences of earthly actions. His actions are driven by divine justice and mercy, ensuring a fair and equitable judgment for all. This is detailed in several apocryphal texts.

keyMyths.title: The Guidance of Souls
keyMyths.description: Remiel is also described as guiding souls to judgment. He leads the newly resurrected individuals through the process of assessment, ensuring they are properly prepared for their eternal fate. This task requires immense compassion and understanding, as he witnesses the full spectrum of human experience and the consequences of choices made during life. His guidance is intended to provide comfort and clarity during a time of immense uncertainty, helping souls navigate the transition to the afterlife. This is alluded to in various Kabbalistic texts.

keyMyths.title: Remiel and Divine Visions
keyMyths.description: In some traditions, Remiel is associated with divine visions and the interpretation of dreams. He is believed to grant individuals glimpses into the future and provide insight into the mysteries of the universe. This role connects him to the prophetic tradition, empowering individuals to understand God's will and purpose. Remiel's presence in these visions often signifies a period of profound transformation and spiritual growth, guiding individuals towards a deeper understanding of themselves and their place in the cosmos. The Book of Enoch hints at this capability.

associations.significance: Oversees the resurrection of the dead.

worshipPractices.name: Invocation
worshipPractices.description: Remiel is primarily invoked for guidance in understanding divine visions and for assistance in preparing for judgment. Prayers often seek his intercession for mercy and a favorable outcome in the afterlife. Some practitioners also seek his help in understanding dreams and gaining spiritual insights.

festivals.name: None Established
festivals.description: Due to Remiel's status outside mainstream religious canon, no established festivals or holy days are specifically dedicated to him. However, some individuals may choose to honor him during periods of reflection on life, death, and the afterlife.

cultural.modernLegacy: Remiel's modern legacy is primarily within esoteric and occult circles, where he is often invoked for his association with resurrection, visions, and judgment. He appears in some works of modern fiction and is sometimes included in lists of angels in New Age spirituality. His name is also occasionally used in symbolic or metaphorical contexts.

extendedContent.title: Origins & Birth
extendedContent.content: Remiel's origins are shrouded in the apocryphal and pseudepigraphal texts of Judaism and Christianity, meaning he is not found in the generally accepted canon of either religion. His name, translating to "Thunder of God" or "Mercy of God," suggests a powerful and compassionate divine being. He appears in texts such as the Book of Enoch and the Apocalypse of Baruch, where he is depicted as an archangel with significant responsibilities related to the afterlife and divine judgment. Unlike angels such as Michael or Gabriel who have clearly defined roles and frequent mentions across multiple accepted religious texts, Remiel's position is less defined, and his stories are more varied depending on the specific text being referenced. The exact circumstances of his "birth" or creation are not described, but as an archangel, he is assumed to be a creation of God for specific divine purposes.

extendedContent.title: Major Myths & Stories
extendedContent.content: Remiel features in several apocryphal narratives, most notably in 1 Enoch, where he is described as one of the angels who presides over those who rise. He is also said to be the angel who is "over true vision". In 2 Baruch, Remiel is responsible for answering questions posed by Baruch regarding the end times and the fate of souls. One significant aspect of Remiel's role is his association with the resurrection of the dead and the guidance of souls to judgment. He is often depicted as one who oversees the process, ensuring that souls are properly assessed and directed to their appropriate destinations. Unlike some archangels who engage in direct battles against evil, Remiel's role is more administrative and guiding, focusing on the processes of judgment and resurrection, thus providing an important function in divine governance.

extendedContent.title: Legacy & Influence
extendedContent.content: Remiel's legacy is primarily confined to esoteric and occult traditions, where he is invoked for his connection to resurrection, visions, and judgment. While not a prominent figure in mainstream religious thought, he holds significance for those interested in angelology and alternative spiritual perspectives. Some modern occultists and New Age practitioners view Remiel as a guide and protector of souls, seeking his assistance in navigating the afterlife and understanding divine messages. His influence can also be seen in some works of modern fiction and fantasy, where he is portrayed as a powerful and enigmatic angel with a unique role in the cosmic order. Overall, while Remiel's influence is not widespread, he remains a compelling figure for those interested in exploring the lesser-known aspects of angelic lore.

shortDescription: Remiel is an archangel in apocryphal Abrahamic texts, associated with resurrection, divine visions, and guiding souls to judgment.
